goal: Lock a reusable 50mm-equivalent hero camera prim aimed at the end-effector with shallow DoF.
thoughts: Full-frame 50mm translates to ~50mm focal length when the sensor is 36mm wide -- configure_camera with explicit focal_length + aperture avoids crop-factor confusion. Named prim so capture is reproducible across renders. Shallow f-stop for end-effector sharp / base just-soft.
code:
cam_path = "/World/Cameras/Hero_Beauty"
create_prim(path=cam_path, type_name="Camera")
configure_camera(
    prim_path=cam_path,
    focal_length_mm=50.0,
    horizontal_aperture_mm=36.0,
    vertical_aperture_mm=24.0
)
set_camera_params(
    prim_path=cam_path,
    f_stop="<FSTOP>",
    focus_distance="<METERS>",
    clipping_range=(0.01, 10000.0)
)
set_camera_look_at(
    camera_path=cam_path,
    target_path="/World/Hero/EndEffector",
    up=(0, 0, 1)
)
capture_viewport(path="<OUT_PNG>", camera_path=cam_path, width=1920, height=1080)
failure_modes: Handing her a Python USD snippet when configure_camera covers the case
Forgetting focus_distance so DoF falls on the wrong plane
Using world up (0,1,0) in a Z-up Isaac Sim stage
